title: Senwannus'gourkahipaff
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Character |type= |image= |name=Senwannus'gourkahipaff |homeworld=[[ThonBoka]]''[[Lando Calrissian and the Starcave of ThonBoka]]'' |birth= |death=[[3 BBY/Legends|3 BBY]], ThonBoka |species=[[Oswaft]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]]''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' |pronouns=He/him |height=Over one [[Kilometer/Legends|kilometer]] across |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes= |skin= |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation= |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Are you ready, old friend?"
"No. Let's go.|Feytihennasraof and Senwannus'gourkahipaff|}} '''Senwannus'gourkahipaff''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Oswaft]] who served as one of three Elders of his [[Realspace/Legends|space]]-dwelling [[Species/Legends|species]] by [[3 BBY/Legends|3 BBY]]. Senwannus'gourkahipaff served in this role when his [[Species/Legends|species]] came under threat by an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]], working with [[Human/Legends|Human]] visitor [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]] to protect the Oswaft from Imperial aggression in the [[ThonBoka]] nebula. Senwannus'gourkahipaff led a large force of his people in using their natural [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] jumping ability to confuse the Imperial fleet into firing on each other, but was killed by a [[Electromagnetic torpedo]] fired by [[Sorcerers of Tund/Legends|Sorcerer of Tund]] [[Rokur Gepta]]. ==Biography== {{Quote|Ah yes, that was the reason you were demonstrating sabacc to us. I had become so fascinated with the game itself, I had quite forgotten that its purpose was explaining why you wished to help us. So, you play a great sabacc game with your own kind out there, and we are a part of it. No, my friend, we do not wish to die, but there seems little alternative. I'll take a card, Starshipmillenniumfalcon, if you please.|Senwannus'gourkahipaff|}} Senwannus'gourkahipaff—known by some as "Sen," for short—was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] member of the massive, [[Realspace/Legends|space]]-dwelling [[Species/Legends|species]] known as the [[Oswaft]], that lived in the region of space called the [[ThonBoka]] nebula. Senwannus'gourkahipaff had become one of three Oswaft Elders by the [[Imperial Era/Legends|Imperial Era]], living into a period of great turmoil for their people. In [[3 BBY/Legends|3 BBY]], the relatively obscure Oswaft came to the attention of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], who felt threatened by the existence of a species that had natural [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] capabilities, although the Oswaft themselves were nonviolent and completely uninterested in events beyond the ThonBoka. The Imperials launched a [[Blockade/Legends|blockade]] intending to starve the Oswaft out. Their only potential salvation ultimately came from an unlikely source—a passing [[Gambling/Legends|gambler]] named [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]] had made an acquaintance with a young Oswaft named [[Lehesu]], and traveled to the ThonBoka in order to help the Oswaft outwit the Empire. Senwannus'gourkahipaff, along with fellow Elder [[Feytihennasraof]], summoned Calrissian to the [[Cave of Elders]]—the only artificial structure in the ThonBoka—only to be immediately charmed by the visiting [[Human/Legends|Human]] over a game of [[Sabacc/Legends|sabacc]]. The group began discussing ways to extricate the Oswaft from their precarious position, when they were interrupted by news that fellow Elder [[Bhoggihalysahonues]] and 1,000 fellow Oswaft had been killed en masse by the Imperial fleet. The mustering of their enemies made the Oswaft's situation mortally dire, and Calrissian quickly formulated a plan to help Senwannus'gourkahipaff's people. Although Senwannus'gourkahipaff originally asserted a moral opposition to fighting back, Calrissian convinced the Elder to aid in a plan to utilize the Oswaft's natural hyperspace jumping capability, and ability to excrete heavy metals in order to create false images, to confuse the Imperial fleet into firing at one another. Leading a force of upwards of one million Oswaft, their deception initially worked brilliantly, causing significant Imperial casualties. However, [[Sorcerers of Tund/Legends|Sorcerer of Tund]] [[Rokur Gepta]]—pursuing his own grudge with Calrissian—arrived in his [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]] ''[[Wennis]]'' and attacked the massed Oswaft with an [[electromagnetic torpedo]] that killed both Elders along with many others of their kind. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|Amazing, you cannot even see the cards, yet you have won hand after hand under fair and impartial conditions. I abase myself to your skill and intellect.|Senwannus'gourkahipaff, to Lando Calrissian|}} Modest and conservative in nature—like most of his species—Senwannus'gourkahipaff could not conceive of one [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] willingly doing harm to another before the Galactic Empire showed up on his doorstep. This extended to self-defense, and though he had no desire to die he initially felt that it was an unavoidable alternative, as leaving the ThonBoka was an unimaginable fate. However, he found himself won over to a new way of thinking upon meeting Lando Calrissian. Senwannus'gourkahipaff became won over to the idea that individual dissent and disagreement could produce new, valuable ways forward, and began to recognize his culture's stagnation. Although games of chance were as foreign to the Oswaft as violence, Senwannus'gourkahipaff delighted in the game of sabacc, and was fascinated by Lando Calrissian's mastery of the game. Over one [[Kilometer/Legends|kilometer]] across in size, Senwannus'gourkahipaff was slightly larger than his fellow Elder Feytihennasraof. ==Behind the scenes== Senwannus'gourkahipaff appeared in ''[[Lando Calrissian and the Starcave of ThonBoka]]'', a novel written by [[L. Neil Smith]] and released in [[1983]]. ==Appearances== *''[[Lando Calrissian and the Starcave of ThonBoka]]'' {{1st}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Senwannus'gourkahipaff |nl=Senwannus'gourkahipaff }} [[Category:Individuals with he/him pronouns]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Oswaft]]
